solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    string baseNeg2(int n) {
        if (n == 0) return "0";
        string result = "";
        while (n != 0) {
            n = -(-n) / 2;
            result = to_string(-n % 2 == 0 ? 0 : 1) + result;
            if (n < 0) {
                n -= 1;
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(log n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The algorithm iteratively divides the number by -2 and adjusts for negative base representation. It builds the result string by appending the remainder at each step. The key strength is its logarithmic time complexity, making it efficient for large inputs. The weakness is the need for careful handling of negative base arithmetic.>
